tests.name: ExampleFirst > abc~
tests.test_code: self.assertEqual(hello(13), ("Hello, World!", 13))

tests.name: ExampleFirst > hello~
tests.test_code: self.assertEqual(hello('Hi'), ("Hello, World!", 'Hi'))

tests.name: ExampleFirstOther > dummy~
tests.test_code: self.assertEqual(hello('Banana'), ("Hello, World!", "Banana"))

tests.name: ExampleFirstOther > hello~
tests.test_code: self.assertEqual(hello(42), ("Hello, World!", 42))

tests.name: ExampleSecond > abc [variation #1]
tests.test_code:
input_data = ['carrot', 'cucumber', 'grass', 'tree']
result_data = [("Hello, World!", param) for param in input_data]
number_of_variants = range(1, len(input_data) + 1)

for variant, param, result in zip(number_of_variants, input_data, result_data):
    with self.subTest(f"variation #{variant}", param=param, result=result):
        self.assertEqual(hello(param), result,
                         msg=f'Expected: {result} but got something else instead.')
